items.title: I was scared to visit Mexico and ended up being electrocuted and thrown in the air – but I loved every minute of it
items.summary: BEFORE I boarded my flight to Mexico for the World Cup, I’ll admit the nerves were kicking in. I had been warned by family and friends to stay safe more there than I had been anywhere else, because if you listen to the outside noise and the negativity, it’s easy to inherit a warped, fearful...

items.title: Swiss overcome penalty 'curse' to oust Colombia
items.summary: Switzerland manager Murat Yakin expressed his delight at breaking the country's penalty "curse" after beating Colombia in a shootout Tuesday to reach the quarterfinals of the World Cup.

items.title: Messi saves Argentina in dramatic win over Egypt
items.summary: Lionel Messi scored in a record sixth straight World Cup knockout match to help Argentina reach the quarterfinals with a stunning late comeback to beat Egypt 3-2 in Atlanta.

items.title: Arbeloa joins Fulham after Real Madrid departure
items.summary: Álvaro Arbeloa has been appointed head coach at Fulham having left Real Madrid last season.

items.title: Egypt bemoan 'injustice' after Argentina defeat
items.summary: Hossam Hassan said Egypt were the victim of an "injustice" in their 3-2 World Cup defeat against Argentina due to FIFA wanting Lionel Messi to "stay in the running."

items.title: U.S. men, women get equal split of $16M WC prize
items.summary: The United States men earned U.S. Soccer $16 million in prize money from FIFA for making the round of 16 at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where the Americans lost 4-1 to Belgium.

items.title: Lautaro told 'emotional' Messi to enjoy 'last WC'
items.summary: Lautaro Martinez told a visibly emotional Lionel Messi to enjoy the moment following Argentina's 3-2 triumph over Egypt on Tuesday to qualify for the quarterfinals of the FIFA World Cup.

items.title: EU lawmakers eye investigating FIFA's Infantino
items.summary: Dozens of European lawmakers are gathering support to launch an investigation in the European Parliament of FIFA boss Gianni Infantino over his involvement in the decision to permit U.S. striker Folarin Balogun to play despite an earlier red card.

items.title: U.S. loss sets betting records; big profits for ho...
items.summary: The United States' run in its home World Cup led to record soccer betting handle and allowed American sportsbooks to profit handsomely when it came to an end.

items.title: 👀 Mahomes, Brunson lead Argentina win reaction
items.summary: The sports world was thrilled by Argentina's epic 3-2 comeback win over Egypt in the World Cup round of 16.

items.title: With World Cup history within its grasp, the U.S. ...
items.summary: It all broke right for the U.S. this tournament: the draw, the injuries, the decisions. And yet America is once again coming to terms with a round-of-16 exit.

items.title: Ahead of Argentina test, Switzerland will look to ...
items.summary: Switzerland continue to write World Cup history after eliminating Colombia and will aim for another memorable achievement against Argentina in the quarterfinals.
